metadata.description: Chinese vocabulary for correspondence, abilities, and expressing hopes

content.name: measure_word_for_letters
content.side_e: Zhège xuéqī wǒ shōudào le sān fēng xìn.
content.side_f: This semester I received three letters.
content.side_g: 封 fēng is the standard measure word for 信 (letters) and sealed items; use 一封信, not 一个信. Also measures emails/telegrams in formal contexts. Don't confuse with 风 fēng 'wind'.

content.name: letter_correspondence
content.side_e: Wǒ zuìjìn xiě le liǎng fēng xìn gěi jiārén.
content.side_f: Recently I wrote two letters to family.
content.side_g: 信 xìn means a physical/written letter; for email prefer 电子邮件 or 邮件. Common verbs: 写信, 收到信, 寄信. Don't confuse with 信 in 相信 (to believe) — same character, different meaning.

content.name: recently
content.side_g: 最近 zuìjìn = 'recently / lately / these days'; covers both near past and near future ('in the coming days'). Usually placed before the subject or right after it; pairs naturally with 怎么样, 忙吗, 好吗.

content.name: most_superlative
content.side_f: I most like listening to music (I like music the most).
content.side_g: 最 zuì is the superlative adverb, placed directly before an adjective or mental verb: 最好, 最喜欢, 最大. No 'than' structure needed — absolute superlative. Contrast with 比较 (relatively) and 更 (even more).

content.name: close_near
content.side_e: Wǒ jiā lí xuéxiào hěn jìn.
content.side_f: My home is very close to school.
content.side_g: 近 jìn = 'near/close' in distance; paired with 离 (A 离 B 近/远). For relationships use 亲近 or 关系近. Opposite is 远 yuǎn. Also appears in 最近 (recently) where 近 metaphorically extends to time.

content.name: school_term_semester
content.side_e: Zhège xuéqī wǒ xué Zhōngwén hé yīnyuè.
content.side_f: This semester I'm studying Chinese and music.
content.side_g: 学期 xuéqī = academic term/semester; 这个学期 / 上个学期 / 下个学期 for this/last/next. Related: 学年 (school year), 寒假/暑假 (winter/summer break).

content.name: in_addition_to_besides
content.side_e: Chúle xué Zhōngwén yǐwài, wǒ hái xué yīnyuè.
content.side_f: Besides studying Chinese, I also study music. (Apart from studying Chinese, further…)
content.side_g: 除了…以外 pairs with 还/也 for inclusion ('besides X, also Y') and with 都 for exclusion ('except X, all…'). 以外 is often dropped in speech: 除了中文，我还学音乐. Keep the second clause's adverb — 还/也/都 is obligatory.

content.name: major_specialty
content.side_g: 专业 zhuānyè is the college 'major/specialty'; for 'profession/job' use 职业 zhíyè. Can also mean 'professional' as an adjective: 很专业 (very professional). Ask majors with 你学什么专业？or 你的专业是什么？

content.name: can_know_how_to
content.side_f: I can speak a little Chinese.
content.side_g: 会 huì expresses learned ability (skills acquired through study): 会说中文, 会开车, 会弹钢琴. Also marks future likelihood: 明天会下雨. Negate with 不会. Contrast with 能 (capacity/permission) and 可以 (allowed).

content.name: later
content.side_f: Later I went to the concert.
content.side_g: 后来 hòulái is narrative 'later/afterwards' and only describes past sequences — do NOT use for future. For future 'later' use 以后 yǐhòu. Often starts a sentence to continue a story.

content.name: concert
content.side_e: Wǒ xīwàng néng qù hěn duō yīnyuèhuì.
content.side_f: I hope I can go to many concerts.
content.side_g: 音乐会 yīnyuèhuì = concert (classical/formal); pop/rock concerts are more often 演唱会 yǎnchànghuì. 开音乐会 = to hold a concert; 听音乐会 / 去音乐会 = to attend one.

content.name: to_hope_hope
content.side_e: Wǒ xīwàng néng yòng Zhōngwén xiě xìn.
content.side_f: I hope I can write letters in Chinese.
content.side_g: 希望 xīwàng works as verb ('to hope') and noun ('hope'). Followed by a clause, not just a noun: 希望你来 (not 希望你). Contrast with 想 (want/think), 愿意 (willing), 盼望 (long for, stronger). Very common with 能 or 可以 inside the hoped clause.

content.name: can_able_to
content.side_e: Wǒ xīwàng hòulái néng xué tán gāngqín.
content.side_f: I hope I can learn piano later.
content.side_g: 能 néng expresses capability (physical/circumstantial) or permission: 我今天能去 (I'm able to go today). Contrast with 会 (learned skill) — 我会开车 vs 我今天不能开车. Negate with 不能. Often paired with 希望/可以.

content.name: to_use
content.side_g: 用 yòng = 'to use / with / by means of'; functions as a coverb introducing the instrument: 用中文写信, 用筷子吃饭. Also noun 'use/usefulness': 有用 / 没用. Don't confuse with 要用 (need to use).

content.name: to_laugh_smile
content.side_a: to laugh at; to laugh; to smile
content.side_e: Bié xiào wǒ, wǒ de Zhōngwén bù hǎo.
content.side_f: Don't laugh at me, my Chinese is not good.
content.side_g: 笑 xiào covers both 'to smile' and 'to laugh'; 笑 + object = 'laugh at/mock': 别笑我. Compounds: 笑话 (joke), 开玩笑 (to joke), 微笑 (to smile). 笑着 + verb = 'doing V while smiling' (e.g., 笑着说).

content.name: to_wish_well
content.side_f: I wish you a smooth semester!
content.side_g: 祝 zhù opens set well-wishes and is followed directly by the recipient + wish: 祝你生日快乐, 祝你学习顺利, 祝你身体健康. Slightly formal/warm; in very close speech people may drop 祝 and just say 生日快乐. Distinct from 住 zhù 'to live'.
